flashcards.q: What are the coordinates of the origin?
Name the horizontal axis in the Cartesian plane.
Name the vertical axis in the Cartesian plane.
How many quadrants are formed by the coordinate axes?
What is the order of numbering the quadrants?
flashcards.a: Anticlockwise (counterclockwise) from the positive X-axis, numbered I, II, III, IV

flashcards.q: What are the sign patterns for coordinates in each quadrant?
Define the term 'abscissa'.
flashcards.a: The x-coordinate of a point; its perpendicular distance from the Y-axis

flashcards.q: Define the term 'ordinate'.
flashcards.a: The y-coordinate of a point; its perpendicular distance from the X-axis

flashcards.q: What is an ordered pair in coordinate geometry?
flashcards.a: A pair of numbers (x, y) written in a specific order, where order matters: (x, y) ≠ (y, x) unless x = y

flashcards.q: Write the general form of coordinates for any point on the X-axis.
flashcards.a: (x, 0), where x is any real number

flashcards.q: Write the general form of coordinates for any point on the Y-axis.
flashcards.a: (0, y), where y is any real number

flashcards.q: What is the y-coordinate of every point on the X-axis?
What is the x-coordinate of every point on the Y-axis?
What is the positive direction of the X-axis called?
What is the negative direction of the X-axis called?
What is the positive direction of the Y-axis called?
What is the negative direction of the Y-axis called?
In which quadrant does a point with both positive coordinates lie?
In which quadrant does a point with negative x-coordinate and positive y-coordinate lie?
In which quadrant does a point with both negative coordinates lie?
In which quadrant does a point with positive x-coordinate and negative y-coordinate lie?
True or False: The origin lies on the X-axis.
True or False: The origin lies on the Y-axis.
True or False: The origin is the only point that lies on both axes.
What is the distance from the origin to any point on the X-axis at coordinate (5, 0)?
What is the distance from the origin to any point on the Y-axis at coordinate (0, 3)?
True or False: (3, 4) and (4, 3) represent the same point.
What determines the position of a point uniquely in the Cartesian plane?
Define the Cartesian plane.
flashcards.a: The plane formed by two perpendicular coordinate axes (X-axis and Y-axis) intersecting at the origin

flashcards.q: Who developed the Cartesian coordinate system?
What is another name for the Cartesian plane?
True or False: A point can lie on both the X-axis and Y-axis at different locations.
flashcards.a: False (only the origin lies on both)

flashcards.q: What are the coordinate axes also called?
How many points can have the same x-coordinate?
flashcards.a: Infinitely many (all points on a vertical line with that x-coordinate)

flashcards.q: How many points can have the same y-coordinate?
flashcards.a: Infinitely many (all points on a horizontal line with that y-coordinate)

flashcards.q: A point is 4 units to the right of the Y-axis. What is its x-coordinate?
A point is 7 units to the left of the Y-axis. What is its x-coordinate?
A point is 5 units above the X-axis. What is its y-coordinate?
A point is 6 units below the X-axis. What is its y-coordinate?
True or False: Axes divide the plane into three regions.
flashcards.a: False (they divide it into four quadrants)

flashcards.q: Which quadrant lies diagonally opposite to Quadrant I?
Which quadrant lies diagonally opposite to Quadrant II?
True or False: Points on axes belong to quadrants.
Define: What is the perpendicular distance of a point from the Y-axis?
flashcards.a: The x-coordinate of the point (its abscissa)

flashcards.q: Define: What is the perpendicular distance of a point from the X-axis?
flashcards.a: The y-coordinate of the point (its ordinate)

flashcards.q: True or False: The coordinate system is unique for each point.
flashcards.a: True (each point has one unique coordinate pair)

flashcards.q: In coordinate (x, y), which value represents the horizontal position?
In coordinate (x, y), which value represents the vertical position?
What is the intersection point of the coordinate axes called?
How are the four quadrants numbered?
flashcards.a: I, II, III, IV starting from the positive X-axis and going anticlockwise

flashcards.q: True or False: A point can have zero as one of its coordinates.
flashcards.a: True (points on axes have one coordinate as zero)

flashcards.q: Write the coordinates of a point on the positive X-axis at distance 8 from origin.
Write the coordinates of a point on the negative Y-axis at distance 5 from origin.
True or False: The coordinate (0, 0) represents a unique point.
What is the main purpose of the Cartesian coordinate system?
flashcards.a: To locate and identify the position of any point in a plane uniquely

flashcards.q: In the Cartesian plane, what is the relationship between the X-axis and Y-axis?
flashcards.a: They are perpendicular to each other

flashcards.q: If a point has coordinates (−3, 4), what is its distance from the Y-axis?
If a point has coordinates (−3, 4), what is its distance from the X-axis?
True or False: The origin is equidistant from all axes.
flashcards.a: True (it has zero distance from both axes)

flashcards.q: Name the two types of axes used in the Cartesian coordinate system.
What is the convention for writing coordinates of a point?
flashcards.a: Abscissa (x-coordinate) comes first, then ordinate (y-coordinate): (x, y)

flashcards.q: True or False: (x, y) = (y, x) for all points.
A point lies on the line where x = 2. How many such points exist?
A point lies on the line where y = −3. How many such points exist?
What type of line is formed by all points with the same x-coordinate?
flashcards.a: A vertical line perpendicular to the X-axis

flashcards.q: What type of line is formed by all points with the same y-coordinate?
flashcards.a: A horizontal line perpendicular to the Y-axis

flashcards.q: True or False: Coordinate axes intersect at right angles.
In Quadrant I, are x-coordinates positive or negative?
In Quadrant III, are y-coordinates positive or negative?
Do points on the X-axis belong to Quadrant I or Quadrant IV or neither?
True or False: Every point in a plane can be represented by coordinates.
